mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS held near zero (0.12%) since 2009, persistently low policy rates continue to depress the equity discount rate and support risk asset valuations, producing a positive drift in forward S&P 500 returns as long as accommodative policy persists.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2015-04-01, near zero lower bound since 2009
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% over a 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month deceleration in PAYEMS growth of more than 50,000 jobs relative to the trailing 6-month average signals weakening labor demand and rising recession odds, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors reprice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change decelerates by more than 50,000 jobs versus its trailing 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 141367 vs revised 141196 as of 2015-04-01
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered deceleration episodes, or Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in RSAFS (retail sales) growth and HOUST (housing starts) growth over a trailing 3-month window signals a synchronized consumer and housing demand slowdown that compounds into broader economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter because neither series alone captures the systemic demand contraction.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST 3-month annualized growth turn negative simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage 926.0 revised to 960.0 as of 2015-03-01
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with slowing consumer momentum
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns remain non-negative in more than 55% of joint-decline episodes, or the interaction term fails to add explanatory power beyond either series individually (incremental Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: During an expansion regime, positive momentum in INDPRO (industrial production) reflects healthy manufacturing supply-side activity that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month; this relationship is expected to be muted or reversed outside expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 105.1786 revised down to 101.3355 as of 2015-04-01, indicating measurement uncertainty
regime_hint: expansion regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO 3-month momentum during confirmed expansion periods (Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows).

mechanism_narrative: Market anticipation of Federal Reserve policy liftoff, proxied by FEDFUNDS beginning to rise from near-zero levels, triggers a repricing of discount rates and increased volatility, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month as investors de-risk ahead of the first rate hike in the cycle.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.10pp over a trailing 2-month window after a prolonged near-zero period
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2015-04-01, with market anticipating 2015 liftoff
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or flat (>=0) in more than 55% of episodes where FEDFUNDS first rises after a near-zero period, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS growth reflects ongoing labor market strength that supports consumer income and spending, generating a modest but persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week regardless of macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised value 141196 thousand persons as of 2015-04-01, consistent with steady job growth
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive PAYEMS prints are not significantly different from returns following negative prints (t-test p>0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between rising CPIAUCSL inflation and a low FEDFUNDS policy rate produces a deeply negative real interest rate environment, which drives capital reallocation into equities as a real-return hedge, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter; this effect requires both conditions jointly since low rates alone or high inflation alone produce ambiguous equity responses.
candidate_trigger: real rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation) falls below -1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised value 235.976 vs vintage 235.74 as of 2015-03-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive relationship with the real-rate proxy (Rank IC < 0.03), or the interaction adds no incremental predictive power beyond FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO exceeding 0.3% during a late-cycle expansion signals an emerging manufacturing contraction that precedes broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as earnings expectations for cyclical sectors deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised down from 105.1786 to 101.3355, showing supply-side measurement volatility
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following an INDPRO decline trigger are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.
